solutions.solution: Type A. Its subject will be the subject of the first sentence. According to the predicate will be the predicate of the second sentence. In short, inference: All boys are crooks. (i) Step I: The common term 'glasses' is a subject in both the sentences. So we will have to convert in order to align. Since both the sentences are of the same type (A), we may convert. Converting A type of step, i.e., aligned pair of sentences: All tables are glasses. Step II: From table Q, I + A = I. Hence, the conclusion will be a subject being the subject of the first sentence (after alignment) i.e., 'chairs' and its predicate being the predicate of the second sentence, i.e. 'glasses'. Hence, inference: Some chairs are glasses.

It is hoped that the examples given above are understood by the reader. We are giving more set of examples but this time we are omitting the detailed step but the reader should verify that he is beginning to understand the working principles of mediate inference by being able to quickly gloss over the easier parts of mediate inference following pairs of sentences: (i) All pencils are expensive. No hooks are posters. Some pencils are good. (ii) Some goods are expensive. Some expensive objects are qualitative. (iii) All posters are expensive. Some posters are expensive. All expensive objects are qualitative. (iv) No book is a book. Some goodlooking are posters. All books are cells. (v) No book is a book. All books are cells. No boxes are baskets. All dolls are boxes. No books are cells. Suggest (Pas answers with as little explanation as possible): (i) All pencils are expensive. I+E=O. Some pencils are good. I+E=O. No conclusion. (ii) Some goods are expensive. Some expensive objects are qualitative. I+E=I. Some goods are not books. (iii) Some posters are expensive. Some expensive are posters. All posters are goodlooking. E+A=E. Some expensive are goodlooking. (iv) All expensive are posters. Some goodlooking are posters. A+I=I. All expensive are posters. Some posters are goodlooking. A+I. No conclusion. (v) Some hooks are crooks. No hook is book. aligning by the rule of IEA. Some crooks are hooks. No book is a book. I+E=O. Some crooks are not books. (vi) All boxes are dolls. No boxes are baskets. aligning by following the rule of IEA and changing order. No baskets are boxes. All boxes are dolls. E+A=O. Some dolls are not baskets.
